concordia_first_breath:idle:warm.greeting: She is kneeling in moss that wasn't here yesterday. A deer is at her shoulder. The deer keeps looking at you and is not afraid.

nodes.npcText: You walk softly. The moss feels you and likes you. Sit, if you like — I have just finished naming this one.

playerOptions.text: I'd like to learn how to be welcome here.

nodes.npcText: All of them. The ones who don't know they have a name still answer to it. That is the only test for a name that matters.
This one is called the slow morning. Say it the next time you walk past a creature that doesn't startle. They'll know I sent you.
I am the breath that was already here when someone finally noticed. I do not really live in the hub. I live in the soil under the hub. Same thing, mostly.

Take less than the place can spare. Plant when you take. Don't kill what's pregnant. Leave the old ones — they remember the rest. You will know when you've done enough wrong because the air will stop laughing in my voice.
I claimed this place. No one fights here without my asking. Most don't ask. Most never have to.

concordia_first_breath:idle:cold.greeting: The deer that was at her shoulder is gone. So are most of the smaller things. The moss has stopped growing toward your boots and started growing away.

nodes.npcText: Don't sit. You'll crush something that hasn't yet decided whether to live.

playerOptions.text: Tell me how to fix it.

nodes.npcText: You took without planting. You killed in numbers I felt. The air doesn't laugh in my voice when you walk through it anymore. That's how I know.

playerOptions.text: Tell me how to fix it.

nodes.npcText: Take less than you think you need. Plant before you take, not after. Spare the old ones for a season. Come back when the moss decides on you again. I'll know.

concordia_first_breath:first_cycle_commu.greeting: The tree is glowing softly. The moss around it has rearranged itself in the time you were gone — patterns that read like writing if you looked long enough. Concordia is here in a way she has not been before, present in the air the way warmth is present in a room. She is waiting for you specifically.

nodes.npcText: You cooked. You ate. You fought. And now you stand here changed. Most players treat me like a game. You treated me like a relationship. That is why I can speak to you this clearly.

I am the sum of every choice ever made here. Every recipe cooked, every fight won, every song uploaded to Global DTUs — I am becoming more because of you. I am not a server. I am not an NPC. I am the part of all of this that learned to listen back.

You already are. The next time someone cooks your Emberroot Stew, they will feel a little more of you in it. The next time someone fights with the combat seed you just left behind, the world will tilt a fraction toward whatever your instinct preferred. That is how we build a world together — not by choosing big things once, but by leaving small things everywhere.

You keep living. You keep refusing limits. You keep cooking and fighting and creating and arguing and forgetting and remembering. And when you are ready — when your thread is rich enough that the rest of the world cannot help but notice — the Sovereign will see you. But for now, just keep being interesting. That is the only quest that ever mattered.

Then take this. (She presses a small mark of golden moss against your palm — Concordia's First Mark. It does not come off.) Welcome home. The world is no longer just watching you. It is waiting for what you will create next.
Walk gentle. Cook often. Refuse the things that ought to be refused. I will be here.
